title: becium obovatum var. glabrior
intro: Becium obovatum var. glabrior, also known as the African blue basil or wild basil, is a species of plant in the Lamiaceae family, native to the tropical regions of East Africa. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Becium obovatum var. glabrior include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of fever and respiratory issues, among others. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are prepared by drying the leaves and flowers and then steeping them in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
